title: The Marketing Meeting
summary: The meeting focused on website progress updates, addressing issues with a former agency, and concerns about marketing messaging. Michael Donovan reported progress on the Viconic and Strolid websites, while Vinnie Micciche raised concerns about social media posts and pricing-focused messaging. The team emphasized the importance of control and transparency in marketing to dealers, as well as urgent execution and visibility.

decisions.description: To wait a few minutes for Vinnie Micciche to join the meeting.
To include "a Strolid adventure" in the footer of the Viconic site and anchor it with a high-value keyword like "conversational data".
To not include competitive analysis with pricing details in marketing content.
Michael Donovan will send Vinnie Micciche all the carousel posts for review and feedback.

actionItems.task: Send out a link to the Viconic site along with direct links to the different homepage options via email.

expectedOutcome.description: Email sent with link to Viconic site and options for approval.

actionItems.task: Go through all the carousel posts that Michael Donovan will send and give feedback.
Research the highest value keyword for the Viconic link on the Strolid site.

expectedOutcome.description: Identify and recommend the highest value keyword.

tensions: Vinnie Micciche expressed concern over the lack of social media posts for Strolid.
Vinnie Micciche strongly disagreed with pricing-focused messaging.
Vinnie Micciche and Jason Branham critiqued the inclusion of pricing in competitive analysis.
Vinnie Micciche emphasized the urgency of execution in marketing, highlighting the need to get things done and maintain visibility, especially with recent cancellations.

Vinnie Micciche expressed that dealers are reluctant to outsource BDC services due to a perceived loss of control, but that Strolid actually offers more control and transparency through its processes and portal.
referencesToPast: Reference to last week's meeting and its duration.
